values: I lent it to someone two seasons ago. They've conveniently forgotten
It was supposed to be a short loan. That was before the snow melted
They said they'd return it after the harvest. Three harvests ago
I need it back. They don't need it. They just haven't bothered
Every time I bring it up they change the subject. I'm done asking nicely
It's mine. They know it's mine. But possession is nine-tenths, apparently
They borrowed it for a project that finished months ago
I've sent messages. I've dropped hints. At this point someone else needs to ask
The thing about lending is you expect to get it back eventually
I gave it freely with the understanding it would come back. The understanding was one-sided
It started as borrowing. Now it's just having. I'd like to reverse that
They've been using it so long they probably think it's theirs
I wouldn't mind so much if they'd asked to keep it. They didn't ask
A friend of a friend has it. Neither the friend nor the friend's friend will return it
The last three times I visited they conveniently weren't home
I lent it during a time of need. The need passed. The item didn't return
They'll give it back if asked by someone they can't avoid at the next market
It's not about the thing. It's about the principle. Actually it's also about the thing
I made it clear it was a loan. They heard what they wanted to hear
I've been too polite about this for too long
The agreement was clear to me. Apparently not to them
They're good people. Terrible borrowers, but good people
I'd go myself but the last conversation about it got uncomfortable
If a stranger asks for it they can't pretend they forgot. They can only pretend with me
It's been long enough that asking feels awkward. But not asking feels worse
I'm starting to think the word 'borrow' means something different where they're from
They keep saying next week. I keep believing them. I'd like to stop believing them
A handshake deal is only as good as the hands involved. One pair was better than the other
I trusted them enough not to put it in writing. My mistake
The polite approach has run its course. I need someone less polite
